title: Recency vs Relevance vs Importance
concept: Weighting retrieved memories by how recent they are, how topically relevant, and how critical the outcome was.

exercises.prompt: What method did we learn about for finding similar past experiences?
exercises.explain: Vector similarity helps us retrieve past experiences that are similar to current ones.

cards.title: Why Different Factors Matter
cards.body: When we want to recall a memory, we should think about three things: how recent it is, how relevant it is to what we're thinking about, and how important it was at that time.

cards.title: Defining Terms
cards.body: Recency refers to how recently an event happened. Relevance means how connected the memory is to our current thoughts. Importance describes how significant the outcome of that event was.

cards.title: Weighing Memories
cards.body: Imagine you have a scale with three plates: one for recency, one for relevance, and one for importance. All three need to be balanced to make the best memory choice!

cards.title: Memory Selection in Action
cards.body: If you're studying for a test and try to recall what you learned last week, the recent memory is likely more helpful than something from a year ago.

cards.title: Misconception Alert
cards.body: Some think older memories are always better. But if a memory is older but not relevant, it might not help us at all!

cards.title: Visualizing Memory Weighting
cards.body: Consider how different factors weigh in on memory retrieval. Recency and relevance can boost importance!

exercises.prompt: What factor helps us choose a memory that is directly related to our current task?
exercises.explain: Relevance directly relates to how connected a memory is to what we're currently thinking or doing.

exercises.prompt: Only the most recent memories are important for retrieval.
exercises.explain: False, because both relevance and importance also affect the memory we retrieve, not just recency.

exercises.prompt: When recalling a memory, we weigh it by recency, __________, and importance.
exercises.sentence: When recalling a memory, we weigh it by recency, relevance, and importance.
exercises.explain: Relevance is the correct term because it indicates the connection of the memory to our current thoughts.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is a factor we consider when retrieving a memory?
exercises.explain: Relevance helps us determine if the memory will assist us in our current situation or goal.

exercises.prompt: When trying to remember an event from last year, which factor should you consider as potentially the least helpful?
exercises.explain: Recency is likely the least helpful in this case because the memory is a year old. The more relevant and important factors are likely to be more useful in your current context.
